item.title: 向我介绍 PHP | Pitch me on PHP
item.description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于新系列的一部分！ 欢迎参与并权衡过去的问题。

假设我以前从未使用过 PHP。 谁能给出该语言的功能以及您为什么喜欢它的原因？ 也可以随意谈谈缺点。

阿纳布罗伊乔杜里—— 马塞洛·巴雷托·尼斯 - Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on May 14

1 Pitch me on Python 2 Pitch me on PHP

Part of a new series! Feel welcome to dip in and weigh in on a past question.

Let's say I've never used PHP before. Can anyone give the run down of what the language does and why you prefer it? Feel free to touch on drawbacks as well.

arnabroychowdhury - May 11

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22

Marcelo Barreto Nees - Apr 22
item.author: ben

item.title: 回到 Lisp 第 1 部分 - 深入了解语言 | Back to Lisp Part 1 - Working Inside the Language
item.description:
我在十几岁的时候发现了计算机程序的结构和解释，并迅速转向 Common Lisp，从z80 宏汇编器到各种Web 框架，以及CAM 系统等有趣的项目。 在我完全没有使用 Lisp 的 12 年之后，我最近决定回去，我对我的发现感到高兴。 这是一系列文章，表达了我对重拾旧爱的想法，并记录了我在此过程中发现的非常实际的事情。

当我在本文中提到 Lisp 时，它指的是我实际使用过的两种语言 Scheme 或 Common Lisp。 您可能可以用 Emacs Lisp 或 Clojure 或任何其他基于 SEXP 的语言来替换它们，然后也照做。 我讨厌人们写 LISP 大写，就好像我们仍在使用 60 年代的东西一样，我将使用 Lisp 来传达现代风格。

在第一篇文章中，我将讨论我最怀念的东西：在语言中工作。

虽然许多语言都提供 REPL（读取 eval 循环，即可以用来执行语句的提示），但很少有人将它作为与软件系统交互的中心方式。

笔记本风格的界面如今非常普遍（我想到了 python 笔记本、R studio 和 Wolfram Mathematica），但它们更多的是关于“会话”和“文档”，关于探索和剖析数据，而不是大规模编程。 其他系统通常带有一个调试控制台，您可以使用它在运行时检查和修改系统（想想浏览器 javascript 控制台）。 最后，可以快速执行的临时文件在 IDE 中很常见。

在 Lisp 项目中，您在文件中编写函数、模块和包，这在编程项目中很常见，但您始终让编译器运行，编译您编写的内容并就您输入的内容提供反馈。 在传统的 IDE 中，IDE 对程序的理解与执行环境脱节（要么在 IDE 本身中实现，要么在单独的语言服务器进程中运行）。 使用 Lisp，编译器充当 LSP 来帮助您与代码交互（去定义、检查等），作为运行实验的快速提示，作为调试器来跟踪/调试/检测并与实际运行的系统交互，作为外壳来管理您的包、部署、构建和运行时系统。

使用 Lisp，一切都感觉紧密（且牢固）连接在一起。

作为语言创建的编程是明确的

编程就是让计算机为我们做事。 但是计算机只真正关心它们的 CPU 可以执行的指令。 由于我们的大脑无法理解汇编语言流，因此我们创建了编程语言、连贯的、可读的组合单词和概念的方式，以便我们一方面可以在人类之间进行协作，另一方面让计算机执行我们的想法。

编程语言（如 python、javascript 等）、库、框架、命名约定、设计模式都有助于创建项目工作人员共享的“编程方言”。 这种语言使我们能够以一种计算机可以执行且同事可以理解的方式表达我们试图解决的问题的解决方案。 更有针对性的项目语言通常被称为“领域特定语言”。

这些方言由以下因素塑造：

  • 使用的框架和库（即，我们使用 react 和 redux）
  • 使用的设计模式（我们为全局存储使用高阶组件和上下文提供程序）
  • 代码和命名约定（我们将我们的处理程序称为 onX，我们的存储操作的形式为 verbObjectObject。我们将 immer 用于类似命令式的存储减速器）

使用的编程语言在语法方面或多或少是灵活的：特定领域的语言通常必须携带语法包袱来表达某些概念。 例如，您可以通过使用转换类、状态枚举和事件调度的某些命名约定来实现状态机。 我认为 Javascript 之所以如此成功，是因为它是一种语言，可以很容易地通过语法进行创造性和优雅。

为概念找到令人满意的 API、语法和命名约定可能非常困难。 与底层编程语言的阻抗不匹配也可能意味着错误比实际应该更容易制造。 在转译或使用高级元编程技术时，运行时错误通常很难映射回原始代码。 方言仍然感觉像方言，是底层编程语言的修改、生活、混蛋版本。

这为您提供了一个更简单的工具，不仅可以创建编程方言，还可以实际修改底层语法以更简洁地表达有用的概念。

这是一把双刃剑，因为很容易创建带有难以理解的语法扩展的不连贯的项目语言。 一个项目通常最多需要一个或两个语法扩展来支持它的项目语言，而这些通常是微不足道的（例如，一种定义状态机枚举的简单方法）。 在传统语言中，巧妙的闭包模式或一些代码生成也可以帮助您实现目标。

然而，Lisp 的美妙之处在于构思阶段。 很容易尝试不同的语法思想，在元和实用级别之间无缝移动，在 REPL 中运行实验，按摩语法。 这使得可以快速了解项目的基本概念是什么，并简洁地表达它们。

它允许我们使用许多巧妙的技巧来制作看起来几乎像语言的方言。 多年来，在 Javascript 中尝试了很多东西和模式：带有 lodash 和下划线的函数式链，带有 redux 和 react 钩子的函数式反应式编程，带有反应钩子的效果编程等等我从来没有接触过。 当然，Javascript 也是一个广泛使用的转译目标，为核心语言带来了有趣的创新，同时仍然保持了密切的相似性。 例如，源映射是使 Javascript 方言在实践中有用的重要工具。

我错过了在语言层面尝试概念

我过去使用 Lisp 的经验深深地根植了这种“用语言思考”的方式。 即使在编写 PHP、Javascript 或 C++ 时，我也会遇到我在 Lisp 中探索过的模式和想法。 虽然我无法转换我正在使用的语言，但我可以构建一种具有良好语法基础的方言，因为我在 Lisp 中将它构建为一种“真正的”语言。 我使用我所拥有的东西（语法技巧、命名约定、API 设计）来使其尽可能优雅且在计算上无懈可击。

随着时间的推移，我忘记了使用 Lisp 来尝试不同的方法是多么容易。 用 C++ 设计一种并发任务语言需要很多代码行和很多仔细的思考。 虽然您可以使用宏和代码生成非常快速地勾勒出一些东西，或者通过熟悉 C++ 模板，但您仍然与大量语法和操作复杂性作斗争。

在 Lisp 语言中，您可以通过编写您希望编写的程序进行试验，然后在 3 个宏中实现它，然后运行它，在 REPL 中打印出 AST 以进行调试。 为并发数据流构建语法是一个下午的项目。

尽管我目前的主要语言（PHP、Javascript 和 Golang）根本不是 Lisps，但我重新连接了创建语言的想法，并将继续尝试一些概念，一旦改进后我就可以移植过来。



原文:

I discovered Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs in my late teens and quickly moved on to Common Lisp, working my way up from a z80 macro assembler to various web frameworks, and fun projects like a CAM system. After 12 years where I didn't work with Lisp at all, I recently decided to go back, and I am delighted by what I found. This is a series of articles that articulate my thoughts about coming back to an old love, and document the very practical things I found along the way.

When I mention Lisp in this article, it will refer to either Scheme or Common Lisp, the two languages I have actually used. You can probably replace them with Emacs Lisp or Clojure or any other SEXP based language and follow along just as well. I hate it when people write LISP uppercase as if we were still using something from the 60ies, I'm going with Lisp to convey a modern touch.

In this first article, I will talk about what I missed most: working inside a language.

REPL programming is the foundation

While many languages offer a REPL (read eval loop, i.e. a prompt you can use to execute statements), few adopt it as the central way to interact with your software system.

Notebook-style interfaces are pretty common these days, (python notebooks, R studio and Wolfram Mathematica come to mind), but they are more about "sessions" and "documents", about exploring and dissecting data than large scale programming. Other systems often come with a debug console you can use to inspect and modify the system at runtime (think browser javascript console). And finally, scratch files that can quickly be executed are common in IDEs.

In Lisp projects, you write functions and modules and packages in files, as is usual in programming projects, but you always have the compiler running along, compiling what you write and giving you feedback on what you typed. In traditional IDEs, the IDEs understanding of the program is divorced from the execution environment (either by being implemented in the IDE itself, or being run in a separate Language Server Process). With Lisp, the compiler functions as LSP to help you interact with your code (go to definition, inspect, etc...), as quick prompt to run experiments, as debugger to trace / debug / instrument and interact with the actual running system, as shell to manage your packages, deployments, builds and runtime systems.

With Lisp, everything feels intimately (and robustly) connected.

Programming as language creation is explicit

Programming is about getting computers to do things for us. But computers only really care about instructions that their CPU can execute. Since our brains can't comprehend streams of assembly language, we created programming languages, coherent, readable ways of assembling words and concepts so that we can collaborate amongst humans on the one side, and have computers execute our ideas on the other.

Programming languages (as in python, javascript, etc...), libraries, frameworks, naming conventions, design patterns all contribute to create a "programming dialect" shared by people working on a project. This language allows us to express solutions to the problems we are trying to solve in a way that is both executable by a computer, and understandable by our colleagues. More targeted project languages are often called "Domain Specific Languages".

These dialects are shaped by:

  • frameworks and libraries used (i.e., we use react and redux)
  • design patterns used (we use higher order components and context providers for a global store)
  • code and naming conventions (we call our handlers onX, and our store actions are of the form verbObjectObject. we use immer for imperative-like store reducers)

The programming language used can be more or less flexible in terms of syntax: domain-specific languages often have to carry syntactical baggage around to express certain concepts. For example, you might implement state machines by using classes for transitions, enums for states, and certain naming conventions for event dispatching. Javascript I think is so successful because it is a language that makes it easy to be creative and elegant with syntax.

Finding a satisfying API, syntax and naming conventions for concepts can be tremendously difficult. Impedance mismatch with the underlying programming languages can also mean that bugs are easier to make than they really should. When transpiling or using advanced meta programming techniques, the runtime errors are often hard to map back to the original code. Dialects still feel like dialects, modified, lived, bastardized versions of the underlying programming language.

Lisp languages don't really have much in way of syntax, as you usually write the program in terms of nested linked lists representing the abstract syntax tree. This gives you a much more simpler tool to not only create a programming dialect, but actually modify the underlying grammar to allow for a much more concise expression of useful concepts.

This is a two edged sword, as it is easy to create incoherent project languages with inscrutable grammatical extensions. A project usually needs at most one or two grammatical extensions to support its project language, and these are usually trivial (for example, an easy way to define state machine enums). In traditional languages, a clever closure pattern or some code generation will get you there just as well.

The beauty of Lisp however is during the ideation phase. It is very easy to try out different syntax ideas, move seamlessly between the meta and the practical level, run experiments in the REPL, massage syntax. This makes it possible to quickly home in on what fundamental concepts for the project are, and expressing them succinctly.

Javascript is an interesting language because it is very malleable. It allows us to use many clever tricks to make dialects that look almost like languages. Over the years, many things and patterns have been tried in Javascript: functional chains with lodash and underscore, functional reactive programming with redux and react hooks, effect programming with react hooks and many more I have never about. Of course, Javascript is also a widely used transpilation target, bringing interesting innovations to the core language while still maintaining close similarity. Source maps for example are an essential tool in making Javascript dialects useful in practice.

I missed experimenting with concepts at the language level

My past experience with Lisp has deeply engrained this way of "thinking in languages". Even when programming PHP, Javascript or C++, I will encounter patterns and ideas that I have explored in Lisp. While I can't transform the language I am working with, I can build a dialect that has sound grammatical foundations, because I built it as a "real" language in Lisp. I work with what I have (syntax tricks, naming conventions, API design) to make it as elegant and computationally airtight as possible.

Over time, I forgot how easy it was to use Lisp to experiment with different approaches. Designing a concurrent task language in C++ takes many lines of code and a lot of careful thought. While you can sketch things out pretty quickly using macros and code generation, or by being well acquainted with C++ templates, you still wrestle with a lot of syntax and operational complexity.

In a Lisp language, you can experiment by writing a program as you wish you could write it, then implementing it in 3 macros and then running it, printing out ASTs in the REPL for debugging. Building a grammar for concurrent data streams is an afternoon project.

Even though my main languages currently (PHP, Javascript and Golang) are not Lisps at all, I reconnected with this idea of creating languages, and will keep on experimenting with concepts that I can then port over once refined.
item.author: wesen

item.title: 2022 年设置 Redis 的最简单方法 | The Easiest Way to Set Up Redis in 2022
item.description:
您是否一直在努力设置 Redis？

或者您是否正在寻找一种更简单的方法来启动和运行它？

无论哪种方式，您都可能想查看这个免费的简单 Redis 设置，它可以节省您的时间！

如果您不知道 Redis 是什么以及它的用途，请不要担心。 这是一个简短的大纲，可以帮助您更好地理解它。

在讨论设置方法之前，让我们快速浏览一下 Redis。

简而言之，Redis 是一个开源的内存数据存储，可以用作缓存、数据库和消息代理。 最重要的是，它支持各种数据结构。

图片来自Fireship.io

在数据库世界中，Redis 被认为是革命性的。

它是内存中、无模式设计 (noSQL) 与可根据您的数据需求定制的高性能、多功能数据结构和模块的令人印象深刻的混合体。 除了将数据存储在内存中之外，Redis 还通过备份和复制实现了高持久性和可用性。

您可能希望使用 Redis 实现高度可用的内存缓存，以：

  • 减少数据访问延迟
  • 提高吞吐量
  • 最小化 SQL 或 NoSQL 数据库以及应用程序本身的负载

好的，是时候设置 Redis 了！

启动和运行 Redis 的最简单方法是使用 Redis Enterprise Cloud。

这种方法降低了遇到操作系统特定问题的可能性，例如 Windows 对 Redis 的支持不足。 它还允许您在任何地方访问云数据库端点，无论您使用的是其他计算机还是虚拟机。

流程简述

  1. 在 Redislabs 上注册一个帐户。
  2. 添加新订阅。
  3. 创建一个新的数据库。
  4. 选择协议和模块。
  5. 检索数据库信息。

首先，您需要一个 Redislabs 帐户才能设置数据库。

值得庆幸的是，他们的注册过程很轻松，因为您可以使用您的 Google 帐户或 GitHub 帐户进行注册！ 如果两者都没有，可以填写注册表。

对于此示例，我使用 GitHub 注册一个 Redislabs 帐户。

在设置数据库之前，您需要有一个订阅计划。 单击New subscription 。

然后，选择固定计划。

选择您要使用的云供应商。 对于此示例，我使用的是 AWS。

您还需要选择您希望服务位于哪个区域，选择最接近您的应用程序托管位置的区域。

对于免费计划，您可以获得 30MB 的内存存储，并且没有复制/高可用性。

接下来，选择一个对您的用例有意义的订阅名称（例如您的应用程序名称）。 然后，单击Create subscription 。

您现在应该有一个订阅号 (Subscription #1715073) 和创建新数据库的选项。

现在，您可以继续创建一个新数据库！

您可以通过单击屏幕右上角或中心的New database来执行此操作。

选择一个适合您使用它的数据库名称（例如您的应用程序名称）。

选择Redis作为您的数据库协议。 至于您将使用的模块，您可以同时选择RediSearch和RedisJSON 。 这两个模块通常对于大多数项目来说已经足够了。

填写完数据库创建表单后，单击Activate database 。 之后，您只需要等待 Redislabs 完成设置您的数据库。

创建数据库后，您可能想知道应该如何连接 Redis 数据存储。

您可以在新创建的数据库上查找Public endpoint信息。

记下 Redis 主机名和端口号。

对于此示例，我有redis-19827.c83.us-east-1-2.ec2.cloud.redislabs.com:19827的公共端点。 主机名是redis-19827.c83.us-east-1-2.ec2.cloud.redislabs.com ，端口号是19827 。

注意：您可能还需要密码才能连接到新创建的 Redis 数据库。

  1. 进一步向下滚动数据库信息页面。
  2. 转到Security部分。
  3. 检索您的Default user password 。

虽然您可以使用默认凭据来连接 Redis 数据库，但最好稍后将其更新为更安全的凭据。

您可以通过单击Edit database并更改您想要更新的信息来完成。

恭喜！ 您现在应该已经成功设置了 Redis Cloud 数据库。

常见问题 (FAQ)

我可以将 Redis 与哪些编程语言一起使用？

这些包括（但不限于）Node.js、PHP、Java、Python、Ruby、C#、C++ 等。

检查您的语言和框架的库或包以使用 Redis。 例如，Node.js 具有用于与 Redis 数据库交互的node-redis npm 包。

我要为此 Redis 设置付费吗？

如果您选择免费的固定订阅计划，则不会向您收费。

但是，免费计划确实有其限制，例如 30MB 存储空间、缺乏复制、最多 30 个连接等等。 通常，这对于本地开发需求来说已经足够了，所以不用太担心。

你不能在 Docker 上安装和运行 Redis 吗？ 这比点击 Redislabs 上的所有 GUI 更快。

本教程旨在提供设置 Redis 的最简单方法。

对于刚开始的初学者来说，这个中间步骤可能会令人生畏。

资源/推荐阅读：

学习 Redis 的有趣资源：

  • 免责声明：

    这是一份入门指南，旨在帮助初学者轻松设置 Redis。 本指南仅表达我的想法和意见（基于我有限的知识），绝不能替代实际参考。

    如果我犯了错误或您不同意，我将不胜感激评论中的更正和讨论！

    其他媒体

    请随时通过其他媒体与我联系！



原文:

Have you been struggling with setting up Redis?

Or are you looking for an easier way of getting it up and running?

Either way, you might want to check out this FREE simple Redis setup that will save you time!

If you don't know what Redis is and what it is used for, don't worry. Here is a brief outline that will help you understand it better.

Redis: Multipurpose In-memory Data Storage

Let's go over a quick overview of Redis before discussing the setup method.

In short, Redis is an open-source, in-memory data store, that can serve as a cache, database, and message broker. On top of that, it supports various data structures.

Image from Fireship.io

In the database world, Redis is considered revolutionary.

It is an impressive hybrid of in-memory, schema-less design (noSQL) with high-performance, versatile data structures and modules that can be tailored to your data needs. On top of storing data in-memory, Redis allows for high durability and availability through backups and replication.

Redis is one of the most adept, optimal, multi-purpose databases out there: It scales horizontally like a key-value data store, but it also provides various functionalities, despite its simplicity.

You would want to use Redis to implement a highly available, in-memory cache to:

  • reduce data access latency
  • improve throughput
  • minimize the load on your SQL or NoSQL database and on your application itself

Alright, time to set up Redis!

Redis Cloud Setup

The easiest way of getting Redis up and running is by using Redis Enterprise Cloud.

This method reduces the likelihood of running into operating system-specific issues, such as the lack of Windows support for Redis. It also allows you to reach the cloud database endpoint anywhere, whether you are using other computers or VMs.

Short Outline of the Process

  1. Sign up for an account on Redislabs.
  2. Add a new subscription.
  3. Create a new database.
  4. Choose a protocol and modules.
  5. Retrieve database information.

1. Redislabs Account Registration

First, you need a Redislabs account to be able to set up a database.

Thankfully, their registration process is hassle-free, since you can just sign up with either your Google account or GitHub account! If you have neither of the two, you can fill in the registration form.

For this example, I'm using GitHub to register for a Redislabs account.

2. Add New Subscription

Before you can set up a database, you'll need to have a subscription plan. Click New subscription.

Then, choose fixed plans.

Select the cloud vendor that you want to use. For this example, I'm using AWS.

You also need to choose which region you want the service to be located in, choose the closest one near where your application will be hosted.

For the FREE plan, you get 30MB of in-memory storage and no replication/high-availability.

Next, choose a subscription name that makes sense for your use case (e.g. your app's name). Then, click Create subscription.

You should now have a subscription number (Subscription #1715073) and the option to create a new database.

3. Create New Database

Now, you can proceed to create a new database!

You can do this by clicking New database on either the top-right of your screen or at the center.

Choose a database name that suits what you are doing with it (e.g. your app's name).

4. Choose Database Protocol and Modules

Select Redis as your database protocol. As for the modules you'll be using, you can choose both RediSearch and RedisJSON. These two modules are usually enough for most projects.

Click Activate database once you are done filling out the database creation form. After that, you just need to wait for Redislabs to finish setting up your database.

5. Retrieve Database Information

After the database is created, you're probably wondering how you're supposed to connect with the Redis data store.

You can look for the Public endpoint information on your newly created database.

Take note of the Redis hostname and the port number.

For this example, I have the public endpoint of redis-19827.c83.us-east-1-2.ec2.cloud.redislabs.com:19827. The hostname is redis-19827.c83.us-east-1-2.ec2.cloud.redislabs.com and the port number is 19827.

Note: You may also need a password to connect to your newly created Redis database.

  1. Scroll further down the database information page.
  2. Go to the Security section.
  3. Retrieve your Default user password.

While you can use the default credentials to connect your Redis database, it is best to update it to a more secure one later on.

You can do it by clicking the Edit database and changing the information that you'd like updated.

Congratulations! You should have a Redis Cloud database successfully set up now.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Which programming languages can I use Redis with?

Redis supports most modern programming languages. These include (and are not limited to) Node.js, PHP, Java, Python, Ruby, C#, C++, and so on.

Check your language and framework's libraries or packages for utilizing Redis. For example, Node.js has the node-redis npm package for interacting with a Redis database.

Am I going to be charged for this Redis set up?

If you choose the free, fixed subscription plan, you will not be charged.

However, the free plan does come with its limitations such as 30MB storage, lack of replication, 30 maximum connections, and so on. Usually, this is enough for local development needs, so don't worry about it too much.

Can't you just install and run Redis on Docker? That would be faster than clicking through all the GUI on Redislabs.

This tutorial aims to provide the easiest way to set up Redis.

One issue with a Docker set up is that it requires someone to know (or learn) how to configure Docker images and containers. This intermediate step can be intimidating for beginners who are just starting out.

Resources/Recommended Reading:

A fun resource for learning Redis:

  • Fireship's Video on Redis

DISCLAIMER:

This is a Starter Guide meant for helping beginners easily set up Redis. This guide only expresses my thoughts and opinions (based on my limited knowledge) and is in no way a substitute for actual references.

If I ever make a mistake or if you disagree, I would appreciate corrections and discussions in the comments!

Other Media

Feel free to reach out to me in other media!

item.author: snowciel

item.title: #13：在静态 Web 应用上托管 PWA | #13: Hosting PWA on Static Web Apps
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 2, Day 6天！

昨天我们讨论了在静态 Web 应用上部署 Blazor 应用。 今天让我们继续介绍您可以在 SWA 上成功托管的另一种类型的项目 - 渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA)。

我们将涵盖的内容

关于作者

他定期就渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA) 和 Angular 发表演讲。 关注他@webmaxru或在 dev.to 上

渐进式 Web 应用程序

渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA)是一种 Web 前端应用程序，它使用开放式 Web 技术逐步增强，以确保它根据可用功能在每台设备上提供最佳体验。 您的应用程序具有“渐进式”标题的“基线”是它的可安装性和离线就绪性。 我们跳过“使用 https 提供服务”的要求，因为它已成为当今任何 Web 应用程序的基准。

如果您想了解这些技术，欢迎您通过#30DaysOfPWA项目的核心概念部分来了解 PWA 基础知识 - 请参阅上面的路线图，或在 dev.to 上赶上该系列：

我还推荐以下有关使用 Workbox 6 自动化服务人员的视频，以快速入门介绍 Workbox。

在 Azure 静态 Web 应用上托管 PWA 与“经典”应用不同吗？

渐进式 Web 应用程序仍然是一个 Web 前端应用程序，因此 SWA 资源的初始脚手架是相同的 - 您已经从 #30DaysOfSWA 的前几章中学习了如何部署和配置各种类型的 Web 应用程序。 但是为了确保您的“SWA 上的 PWA”在在线和离线时都能按预期工作，以提供最佳的用户和开发人员体验，您可能需要特别注意一些实现细节并微调一些配置选项。 我们将在本章中介绍以下几点：

  • 构建您的服务工作者作为整个应用程序构建的一部分
  • 设置 HTML5 路由 - 用于单页应用程序 (SPA) 用例
  • 为 SWA 托管的 API 应用缓存策略
  • 显示自定义错误页面
  • 如何处理 PWA 中的身份验证

示例项目

我在 SWA Starter 项目上创建了一个 PWA，并实施了所有最佳实践。 该应用程序托管在 SWA 上，如下所示。 让我们来探索一下！

技术栈：

  • 由create-react-app工具搭建的 React 应用程序。 一个简单的 SPA，有一些路由和调用 API 端点的可能性。 请注意，所描述的方法适用于任何 Web 前端应用程序：基于框架的（包括您的自定义框架）或“vanilla” JavaScript 。 当您必须在部署之前对源代码执行一些操作（“构建应用程序”）时，我们使用 React 只是为了更接近现代前端应用程序开发的真实场景。
  • 由Workbox库提供支持的服务工作者。
  • 在 SWA 资源中创建的两个 API 端点 - 模拟“动态”（ /api/news ）和“保守”（ /api/archives ）数据源。

构建您的服务工作者作为整个应用程序构建的一部分

我们在应用程序的src/sw文件夹中的 service worker 文件还没有准备好部署。 我们必须

  1. 由workbox-build模块处理它以填充要预缓存的资源（以提供离线准备）
  2. 捆绑它
  3. 选择生产或开发模式
  4. 缩小部署

这就是为什么在package.json的scripts中我们有"build-sw": "node scripts/sw-build.js && npx rollup -c"命令，它正在构建一个服务工作者文件并将其复制到应用程序分发文件夹中。

默认情况下，Azure 静态 Web 应用运行npm run build命令来构建您的应用程序。 所以不要忘记在那里添加一个 service worker build 命令。 结果命令将是：

 "build": "react-scripts build && npm run build-sw"

而不是react-scripts build ，可能有一个命令来使用您选择的框架/捆绑器构建应用程序。 重要提示：保持此顺序 - 您始终在主应用程序之后构建服务工作者，因为将扫描分发文件夹以根据您提供的模式列出要预缓存的文件。

设置 HTML5 路由 - 用于单页应用程序 (SPA)

为了在单页应用程序中正确导航，您必须“要求”网络服务器将所有导航请求重定向到index.html - 以运行应用程序并让它执行路由。 在 SWA 中，您必须在配置文件中有这样的内容：

 "navigationFallback": { "rewrite": "index.html", "exclude": ["/images/*.{png,jpg,gif}", "/static/*"] }

最佳实践：

  • 始终在navigationFallback中尽可能“排除”——它将帮助您识别和修复“找不到资源”错误。
  • 不要将staticwebapp.config.json复制到应用程序分发文件夹 - SWA 会在应用程序源代码中的任何位置找到它。

在 PWA 的情况下，这个后备有一个额外的层：处理导航请求的第一个是服务工作者。 在service-worker.js中，我们必须有以下块才能在 SPA 中正确路由：

 const navHandler = createHandlerBoundToURL('/index.html'); const navigationRoute = new NavigationRoute(navHandler, { denylist: [ new RegExp('^/images/'), new RegExp('^/static/'), ], }); registerRoute(navigationRoute);

上面的配置在在线和离线模式下都可以很好地打开根 url 和像/about这样的“深层链接”。

显示自定义错误页面

最佳实践是创建和显示自定义错误页面（例如 404 Not Found），而不是浏览器中内置的错误页面。 让这些页面尽可能简单是有意义的——无需运行 SPA 本身。 要设置自定义错误页面，请将以下块添加到SWA 配置

"responseOverrides": { "400": { "rewrite": "/400.html" }, "404": { "rewrite": "/404.html" } }

这些页面将自动从 SWA 的导航回退中排除，因为这两个 html 位于应用程序分发文件夹中。 但是对于所需的denylist和显式指令 - 只需将这些地址添加到我们在 HTML5 导航部分遇到的同一个拒绝列表中。

为 SWA 托管的 API 应用缓存策略

使用 Workbox，运行时缓存很简单：您指定要“侦听”和拦截的 url 模式，以及要应用的缓存策略。 有5 种可用策略，您可以随时创建自定义策略。 默认情况下，所有 API url 都以/api开头。 然后在service worker中，我们将使用不同的策略添加这个块来缓存来自我们两个 API 端点的响应：

 registerRoute( ({ url }) => url.pathname.startsWith('/api/news'), new NetworkFirst() ); registerRoute( ({ url }) => url.pathname.startsWith('/api/archives'), new CacheFirst() );

现在，API 中的数据一旦获取，也可以离线使用！

小练习：在 SWA Starter 上打开托管的 PWA ，打开 DevTools 的 Network 选项卡，然后单击“API 缓存”演示区域中的两个按钮。 您认为这两种策略的网络请求/响应模式有何不同？

如何处理 PWA 中的身份验证

这里有几个重要的规则：

  1. 从 Workbox 的导航回退（使用denylist ）中明确排除 SWA 的.auth系统文件夹，并且从不为其设置运行时缓存
  2. 从不运行时缓存用户从受限 API 端点（由 SWA 或外部提供）获取的所有数据

最好的做法是永远不要预先缓存并始终从 service worker 的导航回退中排除与用户身份验证相关的所有 url 和应用程序的受限部分。 查看我们的示例应用程序生成的denylist 。

练习：在 SWA 上部署你的 PWA，离线运行，然后安装！

希望你已经准备好尝试一些有趣的事情来强化你今天学到的东西。

对于本练习：

  • 在 SWA Starter 存储库上分叉 PWA，并使用您在 #30DaysOfSWA 的前几章中获得的技能将其部署到 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序
  • 打开创建 SWA 资源后收到的 url
  • 现在，离线（从 Internet 断开或在 DevTools 中模拟离线模式）并重新加载页面 - 它仍然有效！
  • 使用桌面浏览器地址栏右侧的“可用应用程序”图标，在您的笔记本电脑上安装该应用程序 - 现在它的行为就像一个原生应用程序！

资源

这里有一些资源可以帮助您开始您的 Progressive Web App 之旅

  • 工作箱库


原文:

Welcome to Week 2, Day 6 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

Yesterday we talked about deploying Blazor apps on Static Web Apps. Let's continue today with one more type of the projects you can successfully host on SWA - Progressive Web Apps (PWAs).

What We'll Cover

About the Author

Maxim Salnikov works on the Developer Relations team in Norway, founded #PWASummit #ngVikingsConf and #mobileeraconf, and is a Google Developer Expert on Web Technologies. He speaks regularly on Progressive Web Apps (PWA) and Angular. Follow him @webmaxru or here on dev.to

Progressive Web Applications

A Progressive Web App (PWA) is a web frontend application that is progressively enhanced using open web technologies, to make sure it delivers the best possible experience on every device, based on available capabilities. The "baseline" for your app to have a title "progressive" is its installability and offline-readiness. We skip the "served with https" requirement because it became a baseline for any web app these days.

This chapter of #30DaysOfSWA assumes that you are familiar with the basic concepts of a Service Worker API and automating your service worker with the Workbox library. If you wish to learn about these technologies, you are welcome to go through the Core Concepts part of the #30DaysOfPWA project to learn about PWA fundamentals - see the roadmap above, or catch up on that series here on dev.to:

I also recommend the video below on Automating a service worker with Workbox 6 for a quickstart introduction to Workbox.

Is hosting a PWA on Azure Static Web Apps different from "classic" apps?

A Progressive Web Application is still a web frontend app so the initial scaffolding of your SWA resource is the same - you have learned how to deploy and configure various types of web apps from the previous chapters of the #30DaysOfSWA. But to make sure that your "PWA on SWA" works as intended both online & offline to provide the best possible user & developer experiences, you might need to pay special attention to some implementation details and fine-tune some configuration options. We'll go through the following points in this chapter:

  • Building your service worker as a part of the overall app build
  • Setting up HTML5 routing - for the Single-Page Application (SPA) use-case
  • Applying caching strategies for SWA-hosted APIs
  • Showing custom error pages
  • How to deal with authentication in PWA

Sample project

I created a PWA on SWA Starter project with all the best practices implemented. The app is hosted on SWA as shown below. Let's explore it!

Technical stack:

  • A React application scaffolded by the create-react-app tool. A simple SPA with a few routes and the possibility to call API endpoints. Note that the approaches described are applicable for any web frontend application: framework-based (including your custom frameworks) or "vanilla" JavaScript. We use React only for getting closer to the real-world scenario of the modern frontend app development when you have to perform some operations with your source code ("build the app") before the deployment.
  • A service worker powered by the Workbox library.
  • Two API endpoints created within SWA resource - to emulate "dynamic" (/api/news) and "conservative" (/api/archives) data sources.

Building your service worker as a part of the overall app build

The service worker file we have in the src/sw folder of the application is not ready for the deployment. We have to

  1. Process it by the workbox-build module to populate with the resources to precache (to provide offline-readiness)
  2. Bundle it
  3. Choose production or development mode
  4. Minify for deployment

This is why in package.json's scripts we have "build-sw": "node scripts/sw-build.js && npx rollup -c" command that is building a service worker file and copies it into the app distribution folder.

By default, Azure Static Web Apps runs npm run build command to build your application. So do not forget to add a service worker build command there. The resulting command will be:

"build": "react-scripts build && npm run build-sw"

Instead of react-scripts build, there might be a command to build an application using a framework/bundler of your choice. Important: Keep this order - you always build a service worker after the main application because the distribution folder will be scanned to list the files to precache based on the pattern you provided.

Setting up HTML5 routing - for the Single-Page Application (SPA)

For having correct navigation in your single-page application, you have to "ask" the webserver to redirect all navigation requests to index.html - to run the application and let it do the routing. In SWA, you have to have something like this in the configuration file:

"navigationFallback": {
  "rewrite": "index.html",
  "exclude": ["/images/*.{png,jpg,gif}", "/static/*"]
}

Best practices:

  • Always "exclude" as much as possible in navigationFallback - it will help you to identify and fix "resource not found" errors.
  • Do not copy staticwebapp.config.json to the app distribution folder - SWA will find it in any location within the app source code.

In case of PWA, there is an extra layer of this fallback: the first in a row to handle navigation requests is a service worker. In the service-worker.js we have to have the following block for correct routing in SPAs:

const navHandler = createHandlerBoundToURL('/index.html');
const navigationRoute = new NavigationRoute(navHandler, {
  denylist: [
    new RegExp('^/images/'),
    new RegExp('^/static/'),
  ],
});
registerRoute(navigationRoute);

The above configuration will work fine for opening both root url and "deep links" like /about both in online and offline modes.

Showing custom error pages

It's a best practice to create and show custom error pages (for example for 404 Not Found) instead of the ones built-in in the browsers. And it makes sense to keep these pages as simple as possible - without running a SPA itself. To set up custom error pages, you add the following block to the SWA config

"responseOverrides": {
  "400": {
    "rewrite": "/400.html"
  },
  "404": {
    "rewrite": "/404.html"
  }
}

These pages will be excluded from SWA's navigation fallback automatically because these two html are in the app distribution folder. But for the Workbox and explicit directive needed - just add these addresses to the same denylist we met with the HTML5 navigation section.

Applying caching strategies for SWA-hosted APIs

With Workbox, runtime caching is simple: you specify a url pattern to "listen" and intercept, and a caching strategy to apply. There are 5 strategies available and you can always create a custom one. By default, all API urls start with /api. Then in a service worker, we'll add this block for caching responses from our two API endpoints using different strategies:

registerRoute(
  ({ url }) => url.pathname.startsWith('/api/news'),
  new NetworkFirst()
);

registerRoute(
  ({ url }) => url.pathname.startsWith('/api/archives'),
  new CacheFirst()
);

Now, the data from APIs, once fetched, is also available offline!

Mini-exercise: Open the hosted PWA on SWA Starter, open the Network tab of DevTools, and click on two buttons in the "API caching" demo area. What difference do you see in the network request/response patterns for these two strategies?

How to deal with authentication in PWA

A couple of important rules here:

  1. Explicitly exclude the .auth system folder of SWA from Workbox's navigation fallback (use denylist) and never set up runtime caching for it
  2. Never runtime-cache all data users fetch from restricted API endpoints (served by SWA or external)

And it's a best practice to never precache and always exclude from the service worker's navigation fallback all urls related to the user authentication and restricted parts of your application. Have a look at the resulting denylist of our sample application.

Exercise: Deploy your PWA on SWA, run offline, and install it!

Hopefully, you're ready to try something fun that will reinforce what you learned today.

For this exercise:

  • Fork the PWA on SWA Starter repository and deploy it to Azure Static Web Apps using the skills you gained in the previous chapters of the #30DaysOfSWA
  • Open the url you received after the SWA resource was created
  • Now, go offline (disconnect from the Internet or emulate offline mode in the DevTools) and reload the page - it still works!
  • Using the "App available" icon on the right-hand side of the desktop browser's address bar, install the application on your laptop - now it behaves like a native one!

Resources

Here are some resources to get you started with your Progressive Web App journey into

  • PWA on Microsoft Docs
  • #30DaysOfPWA
  • Workbox library
item.author: nitya

item.title: 我想快速转换 HTML 文件... | I want to convert an HTML file in express...
item.description:
首先，我想让你看到那里。

网页/wiki.html

 <!DOCTYPE HTML> <html> <head> ... </head> <body> <h2> ${tmp.data.name} </h2> <hr> <br> <div id= "article" > ： ： </body> </html>

接下来，我将解释我想要做什么。

我想使用 replace 来读取 HTML 中的字符串“$ {tmp.data.name}”，并使用 Node.js Express 使其成为变量的内容。

然而，

 webdata = String ( webdata ) webdata = webdata . replace ( ' {tmp.data.name} ' , articlesname );

不起作用。 定义了变量。 另外，我不能用字符串而不是变量来尝试它。

请帮我...



原文:

First of all, I want you to see there.

index.js quote

webdata = (fs.readFileSync(__dirname + '/web/wiki.html', 'utf8'));
webdata=String(webdata)
webdata = webdata.replace('{tmp.data.name}',articlesname); res.end(webdata);

web/wiki.html

<!DOCTYPE HTML>
<html>
<head>
...
</head> <body> <h2>${tmp.data.name}</h2> <hr> <br> <div id="article"> ： ： </body>
</html>

Next, I will explain what I want to do.

I want to use replace to read the string "$ {tmp.data.name}" in HTML and use Node.js Express to make it the content of a variable.

However,

webdata=String(webdata)
webdata = webdata.replace('{tmp.data.name}',articlesname);

does not work. Variables are defined. Also, I couldn't try it with a string instead of a variable.

Please help me...
item.author: tsumuri1017

item.title: 如何使用无服务器框架节省 AWS Parameter Store | How to save up on AWS Parameter Store with serverless framework
item.description:
在我们的代码中使用引用 API 密钥或数据库密码的硬编码值是一种不好的做法，甚至存在安全风险。 或者，由于业务决策或不可预见的情况，我们的价值观可能会随着时间而改变； 这意味着每次需要更改值时我们都必须更改代码。 为此，我们可以使用名为 Parameter Store 的 AWS Systems Manager 服务。

我们可以将密码、数据库字符串、Amazon 系统映像 (AMI) ID 和许可证代码等数据存储为参数值。 我们可以将值存储为纯文本或加密数据。

但是，当我们必须使用多个变量时会发生什么？ 维护它们很困难，但是在几个开发环境中问题会加倍。 我们可以轻松达到每个账户和地区 10,000 个标准参数的硬性上限。

我们如何解决这个问题？

我们可以保存几个参数存储； 如果我们开始以 JSON 格式编写它们，我们可以让它们以清晰有序的方式组织起来。

让我们开始使用我们的 JSON 值创建一个 Parameter Store。 我将使用我的名字和姓氏作为此示例的值。

 /dev/myapp/INFO >> {"firstName":"David","lastName":"Llerena"}

使用无服务器框架创建一个 Typescript 无服务器 Lambda 项目。 让我们添加以下依赖项：

 //package.json
{ "name" : "myapp" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "main" : "index.js" , "scripts" : { "test" : "echo \" Error: no test specified \" && exit 1" }, "dependencies" : { "aws-sdk" : "2.952.0" , "@types/aws-lambda" : "^8.10.95" , "serverless" : "^3.15.2" , "serverless-plugin-typescript" : "^2.1.2" , "typescript" : "^4.6.3" }, "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC"
}

现在让我们在名为helperScript.js的文件中创建一些 javascript 代码，创建一个将调用 SSM 服务并读取我们想要解析和读取 JSON 值的每个值的参数存储的函数。 在我们的serverles.yml文件中，我们将引用这个helperScript.js来调用环境语句下我们代码中使用的变量。 授予 lambda 访问 SSM 服务所需的权限。 建议仅授予对我们正在使用的资源的访问权限。

如果我们使用的是版本 3 次要的无服务器版本，我们必须在服务声明下添加此语句variablesResolutionMode: 20210326

 #serverless.yml service : myapp plugins : - serverless-plugin-typescript provider : name : aws runtime : nodejs16.x stage : dev region : us-east-1 environment : FIRST_NAME : ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.firstName} LAST_NAME : ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.lastName} iamRoleStatements : - Effect : " Allow" Action : - ssm:GetParameter - ssm:GetParameters - ssm:GetParametersByPath Resource : " *" custom : prefix : /${self:provider.stage}/${self:service} functions : hello : handler : handler.hello

最后，让我们在 lambda 代码中使用这个 Parameter Store。

 //handler.ts import { Handler } from ' aws-lambda ' ; export const hello : Handler = ( event : any ) => { const response = { statusCode : 200 , body : JSON . stringify ( { message : ' My First name is ' + ` ${ process . env . FIRST_NAME } ` + ' and my last name is ' + ` ${ process . env . LAST_NAME } ` }, null , 2 ), }; return new Promise (( resolve ) => { resolve ( response ) }) }

就是这样！，如果我们执行我们的 lambda，它应该是这样的：

这肯定会帮助我们节省一些参数存储并保持它们整洁。 但是，我们应该考虑这些变量可以获得的大小。 我们可能会考虑使用Advanced Parameters 。

参考： 无服务器框架变量



原文:

It is a bad practice and even a security risk to use hard-coded values within our code that reference an API Key or a Database Password. Alternatively, we can have values that can change over time because of a business decision or an unforeseen situation; this will mean that we have to change our code every time a value needs to be changed. For this, we can use the AWS Systems Manager service called Parameter Store.

Parameter Store is a capability of AWS Systems Manager that provides secure, hierarchical storage for configuration data management and secrets management. We can store data such as passwords, database strings, Amazon Machine Image (AMI) IDs, and license codes as parameter values. We can store values as plain text or encrypted data.

However, what happens when we have to use several variables? It is difficult to maintain them, but the problem doubles with several development environments. We can easily reach the hard cap limit of 10,000 standard parameters per account and region.

How do we solve this?

We can save several parameter stores; if we start writing them in JSON format, we can have them organized in a clear and organized way.

Let us start creating a Parameter Store with our JSON values. I will use my first and last name as values for this example.

/dev/myapp/INFO >> {"firstName":"David","lastName":"Llerena"}

Create a Typescript serverless Lambda Project with serverless framework. Let's add the following dependencies:

//package.json
{ "name": "myapp", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" }, "dependencies": { "aws-sdk": "2.952.0", "@types/aws-lambda": "^8.10.95", "serverless": "^3.15.2", "serverless-plugin-typescript": "^2.1.2", "typescript": "^4.6.3" }, "author": "", "license": "ISC"
}

Now let's create some javascript code in a file named helperScript.js, Create a function that will call SSM service and read a Parameter store which we would like to parse and read each value of the JSON values. ResolveConfigurationProperty statement will reference the stage and name of our service, declared in our serverless.yml file.

//helperScript.js
const SSM = require("aws-sdk/clients/ssm"); module.exports.getParameters = async ({ resolveConfigurationProperty }) => { const prefix = await resolveConfigurationProperty(["custom", "prefix"]); const region = await resolveConfigurationProperty(["provider", "region"]); const ssm = new SSM({ region }); const policy = await ssm .getParameter({ Name: prefix + "/INFO", WithDecryption: true, }) .promise(); const res = JSON.parse(policy.Parameter.Value); return { firstName: res.firstName, lastName: res.lastName };
};

In our serverles.yml file, we will reference this helperScript.js to call the variables used in our code under the environment statement. Give the lambda the necessary permissions to access the SSM service. It is recommended only to give access to the resources we are using.

If we are using a serverless version minor to version 3, we have to add this statement variablesResolutionMode: 20210326 under service declaration

#serverless.yml
service: myapp plugins: - serverless-plugin-typescript provider: name: aws runtime: nodejs16.x stage: dev region: us-east-1 environment: FIRST_NAME: ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.firstName} LAST_NAME: ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.lastName} iamRoleStatements: - Effect: "Allow" Action: - ssm:GetParameter - ssm:GetParameters - ssm:GetParametersByPath Resource: "*"
custom: prefix: /${self:provider.stage}/${self:service}
functions: hello: handler: handler.hello

Finally, let's use this Parameter Store in our lambda code.

//handler.ts
import { Handler } from 'aws-lambda'; export const hello: Handler = (event: any) => { const response = { statusCode: 200, body: JSON.stringify( { message: 'My First name is '+`${process.env.FIRST_NAME}`+' and my last name is '+`${process.env.LAST_NAME}` }, null, 2 ), }; return new Promise((resolve) => { resolve(response) })
}

And that's it!, if we execute our lambda it should look like this:

This will surely help us save up some Parameter Stores and keep them neat and clean. However, we should consider the size that these variables could get. We might take into consideration the use of Advanced Parameters.

Reference: Serverless Framework Variables

item.title: 你不是很一致，是吗？ | You're Not Very Consistent, Are You?
item.description:
蒂娜黄

发表于一致性意味着在保持长期愿景的同时专注于当前手头的任务。

一致性：

  • 允许测量
  • 建立问责制
  • 建立您的声誉
  • 让你相关
  • 维护您的信息

保持一致的好处

  • 反思我们的实践
  • 从事自我保健
  • 问候我们的学习者
  • 保持积极的课堂环境
  • 通过多元文化的视角进行教学
  • 提供有意义和有用的反馈
  • 帮助学生保留信息
  • 培养常规纪律
  • 建立信任的环境
  • 带着同理心和理解倾听
  • 为他人增值
  • 花时间培养与同事和学习者的关系
  • 拥抱安静的时间或正念
  • 激发好奇心并融入创造力的机会
  • 提供探索和探究的时间
  • 参与团队建设以促进沟通和协作
  • 利用社会情感学习
  • 培养一个学习环境，让每个人都了解他们的身份和他们的独特差异

一致性原则

一致性提示

  1. 提醒
  2. 当你完成最小的任务时奖励自己
  3. 立即纠正错误
  4. 使用激励工具继续前进
  5. 给自己时间看看复合变化
  6. 了解您的目标
  7. 知道你有什么时间来实现你的目标
  8. 找出最重要的下一步
  9. 在日程表中写下你的任务
  10. 每天采取行动

快乐编码！



原文:

Tina Huynh

Posted on May 15

Consistency means focusing on the task at hand in the present moment while maintaining a long term vision.

Consistency:

  • allows for measurement
  • creates accountability
  • establishes your reputation
  • makes you relevant
  • maintains your message

"The more you do something, the better you get at doing it. And when you get better, improving your performance gets easier."

“Small disciplines repeated with consistency every day lead great achievements gained over time” — John Maxwell

Benefits from Being Consistent

  • Reflecting on our practice
  • Engaging in self-care
  • Greeting our learners
  • Maintaining a positive classroom environment
  • Teaching through a culturally diverse lens
  • Providing meaningful and useful feedback
  • Helps students retain information
  • Develop the discipline of routine
  • Establish an environment of trust
  • Listening with empathy and understanding
  • Adding-value to others
  • Investing time in cultivating relationships with both colleagues and learners
  • Embracing quiet time or mindfulness
  • Inspiring curiosity and incorporating opportunities for creativity
  • Providing time for exploration and inquiry
  • Engaging in team-building to foster communication and collaboration
  • Leveraging social-emotional learning
  • Foster a learning environment that embraces every individual for who they are and their unique differences

Principles of Consistency

Tips for Consistency

  1. Reminders
  2. Reward yourself when you get even the smallest tasks done
  3. Correct mistakes right away
  4. Use motivational tools to keep going
  5. Give yourself time to see the compound changes
  6. Know the results you are aiming for
  7. Know what time you have available to word towards your goals
  8. Work out what's the most important next step
  9. Write your tasks in your schedule
  10. Take action each and every day

Happy coding!

item.title: 为什么你没有生产力？ | Why you're not Productive?
item.description:
随着我的软件工程之旅的进展，我发现错误和缺点会减慢并降低我的生产力标准。 几年前好像没问题
直到我让自己完成一个自我评估过程！

今天我想分享一下那些常见的滑贴，让你感受一下，并在第一手准备好对抗它们

错误初始化（失去时刻）

作为一个人，我们会对发生在我们身上的一切做出反应。 无论是社交互动还是工作特定。

时间流逝的区域必须是理想的。 作为开发人员，如果给你分配了一个任务，那么应该有一个计划来实施并相处到终点线。

时间永远不会是无限的，所以如果你在正确的时间开始做事，成功的可能性比就会扩大。 你肯定有机会按时提交工作。 每天用 8 小时完成工作是一门艺术。 毫无疑问，您将通过时间和经验了解这一点。

如果您无法做到这一点，那么当您陷入深夜工作支出并确保您的屁股安全时，时间就快到了！
这直接对您的个人生活产生负面影响。 无论是您的家人、朋友还是您自己的空间。

缺乏优先级

主动和被动是开发人员内部的两种性质。 先手的人总是知道未来会发生什么，他/她计划并设置他们的调度程序，而其他人只是拖延。

这种做法导致没有目标成就或低进度水平。 对于那些正在努力寻找工作与生活之间更好契合点的人来说，这也是一个常见的问题。

没用的网上冲浪

开发人员喜欢与全球世界联系，这真的很棒。 但是明智地冲浪而不是盲目地冲浪。 如果您习惯在一个小时内多次滚动您最喜欢的应用程序的提要。 有可能破坏您的生产力栏。 明智地冲浪是指在特定时间冲浪以放松和缓解压力。 当您感到疲倦或沮丧时，观看播客、读书或看电影！

所以这就是现在的全部！

你遇到过这些问题吗？ 或者努力寻找更合适的。 让我知道内部评论，以便我们可以联系线程并一起学习！



原文:

As my software engineering journey progressed, I found mistakes and short comings that slowed and lowered down my productivity bar. Years before, it seemed no problem
until I put myself through a Self Assessment process!

Today I would like to share those common Slippery Patches so you can get a feel and prepare yourself against them on the first hand

False Initialization (Lose the moment)

Being a human, we respond to everything that comes in for us. Either its a social interaction or work specific.

Region of time lapse must be ideal. As a developer, if a task is assigned to you, there should be a plan to implement and get along to finish line.

Time can never be infinite, so if you initiate stuff at the right time the possibility ratio of success broaden itself. You definitely get a chance to submit work at time. Using 8 hrs to complete work daily is an Art. Undoubtedly, you'll learn this by time and experience.

If you are unable to do so, time is near when you get stuck in Late night spendings for work and get your ass safe!
This directly cast a negative impact on your personal life. Either your family, friends or your own space.

Lack of Prioritization

Active and Passive are two natures that live inside developers. The person with first always know what's ahead, he/she plans and set up their schedulers while others just procrastinate.

This practice leads towards No Goal Achievement or Low Progess Levels. Also a common problem of those who are struggling to find a better fit between Work and Life.

Useless Internet Surfing

Developers love to connect with global world and its really awesome. But surf wisely not blindly. If you're use to scroll feed of your favorite app, many times in an hour. Chances are there to spoil your productivity bar. By surfing wisely I mean to surf in a specific time to chill and relieve stress. Watch podcasts, read books or watch a movie when you feel tired or frustrated!

So thats all for now!!

Have you faced these issues? or struggling to find a better fit. Let me know inside comments so we can tie threads and learn together!

item.title: Laravel API 系列：Laravel Sanctum 设置、注册、登录和注销 | Laravel API Series: Laravel Sanctum Setup, Sign Up, Login, and Logout
item.description:
在本系列的这一部分中，您将学习以下内容：

  • 圣所是什么
  • 如何安装和使用 Laravel Sanctum
  • 实现注册功能
  • 实现登录功能
  • 实现注销功能
  • 重组到受保护和公共的路线

在本节中，我将向您展示如何使用 Sanctum 对用户进行身份验证。

安装圣所

由于 Laravel 旨在提供出色的开发人员体验，因此您在本系列的第一部分生成的 Laravel 项目包括 Sanctum，您可以通过前往composer.json文件来确认，它应该在require数组中，如下所示：


绿色框是require数组。 如果您在composer.json文件的数组中找不到 Sanctum，请运行以下命令来安装它：

 composer require laravel/sanctum

上述命令将在您的应用程序中安装 Sanctum，您可以通过再次检查composer.json文件来确认。

创建个人访问令牌迁移

确认 Sanctum 的安装后，接下来是在数据库中创建个人访问令牌表，您可以通过在命令行中运行以下命令发布 Sanctum 的配置和迁移文件来实现：

 php artisan vendor:publish --provider = "Laravel \S anctum \S anctumServiceProvider"

上面的命令将在/database/migrations文件夹中创建一个create_personal_access_tokens_table.php并在/config文件夹中创建一个sanctum.php文件，一旦您验证了这两个文件的创建，接下来要做的就是迁移新的迁移文件，您可以使用以下命令执行此操作：

上面的命令会在你的数据库中添加一个新的personal_access_tokens表，检查你的数据库管理器来验证：

接下来，进入app/Http/Kernel.php文件，将middlewareGroups数组中的api数组替换为以下代码：

 'api' => [ \ Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\EnsureFrontendRequestsAreStateful :: class , 'throttle:api' , \ Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\SubstituteBindings :: class , ],

上面的代码是将用于验证我们的 API 的中间件。

接下来，我将向您展示如何在 Laravel 中保护路由。

保护路线

为了保护您的路由，您需要使用中间件函数对受保护的路由进行分组，如下所示：

 // posts routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { // protected routes go here });

上面的代码在Route Facade 上使用了静态group函数，并添加了中间件数组，该数组利用'auth:sanctum'中间件来保护您在函数内定义的路由。 为了向您展示它是如何工作的，我将在函数内添加所有发布路由，如下所示：

 // posts routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { Route :: resource ( 'posts' , PostController :: class ); Route :: get ( '/posts/search/{title}' , [ PostController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/post/author/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'get_author' ]); });

现在尝试通过向localhost:8000/api/posts发出 GET 请求来获取所有帖子，您应该得到以下结果：

绿色框是您从请求中获得的结果，它显示"message": "Unauthenticated." 路由已成功保护，现在您需要定义用户必须采取的步骤才能获得身份验证。 接下来，我们将定义signup函数。

注意：以上只是一个例子，后面我会重新构建所有的路由。

接下来，我将向您展示如何为与身份验证相关的功能设置控制器。

授权控制器

您在本系列的第二部分中学习了控制器用于组织应用程序中的功能，因此您需要创建一个包含与身份验证相关的所有功能的控制器。

首先，使用 artisan 创建一个控制器，将其命名为 AuthController，如下所示：

 php artisan make:controller AuthController

注意：您不应该添加--resource标志，因为我们不会在这里使用CRUD功能。

这应该创建一个包含以下代码的控制器文件：

 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; class AuthController extends Controller { // }

接下来，添加所需的依赖项，在这种情况下将是：

 use App\Models\User ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; use Illuminate\Http\Response ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash ;

在namespace App\Http\Controllers; 线。

  • 接下来，我将向您展示如何创建注册功能

    报名

    为了让用户能够登录，您需要创建函数。 因此，像这样创建一个公共sign_up函数：

     public function sign_up ( Request $request ){ }

    接下来，验证来自请求对象的数据，如下所示：

     $data = $request -> validate ([ 'name' => 'required|string' , 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email' , 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]);

    上面的代码使用validate函数验证数据。

    • 名称是必需的字符串。
    • 电子邮件是必需的字符串，并且必须是用户表中列内的唯一值。
    • 密码是必填字符串，需要确认，所以用户需要再次输入。

    接下来，使用User模型上的静态create函数创建用户，如下所示：

     $user = User :: create ([ 'name' => $data [ 'name' ], 'email' => $data [ 'email' ], 'password' => bcrypt ( $data [ 'password' ]) ]);

    上面的代码使用带有先前data变量数组的create函数来创建用户。

    注意：上面数组中的password是用bcrypt函数包裹的，所以在将用户保存到数据库之前，密码会被散列。

    接下来，使用$user上的createToken函数生成身份验证令牌，如下所示：

     $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ;

    上面的代码将创建一个令牌，该令牌将与每个请求一起发送到受保护的路由。

    接下来，创建成功创建用户后将发送回的响应：

     $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 );

    上面的代码创建了一个名为$res的变量，它是一个包含创建的user和生成的token的数组，并使用response函数返回它以及状态码201 ，这意味着创建了一个资源，即用户和令牌。 现在sign_up函数应该如下所示：

     public function sign_up ( Request $request ){ $data = $request -> validate ([ 'name' => 'required|string' , 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email' , 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]); $user = User :: create ([ 'name' => $data [ 'name' ], 'email' => $data [ 'email' ], 'password' => bcrypt ( $data [ 'password' ]) ]); $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ; $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 ); }

    接下来，为上述函数创建一个注册路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/signup' , [ AuthController :: class , 'sign_up' ]);

    注意：这条路线将是公开的。

    您现在可以通过将所需数据发送到/signup路由来创建用户，如下所示：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/signup的 URL。
    • 红框是我发送给服务器的form-data格式的数据。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果 - 这将是创建的用户和生成的令牌。

    接下来，将生成的令牌添加为bearer token ，并向您之前定义的受保护路由发送 GET 请求：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即GET请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/posts的 URL。
    • 橙色框是我发送到服务器的令牌类型，即bearer token 。
    • 蓝色框是我发送到服务器的令牌，它是我注册时生成的令牌（这就是您在任何应用程序上注册后自动登录的原因）。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果——这将是数据库中的帖子，因为我没有通过身份验证，所以之前不可用。

    接下来，我将向您展示如何创建登录功能。

    登入

    您需要创建一个登录功能，以便用户可以登录。为此，请创建一个login功能，如下所示：

     public function login ( Request $request ) { }

    接下来，像这样验证请求数据：

     $data = $request -> validate ([ 'email' => 'required|string' , 'password' => 'required|string' ]);
    • 电子邮件是必需的字符串。
    • 密码是必需的字符串。

    接下来，检查用户是否像这样注册：

     $user = User :: where ( 'email' , $data [ 'email' ]) -> first (); if ( ! $user || ! Hash :: check ( $data [ 'password' ], $user -> password )) { return response ([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401 ); }

    上面的代码执行以下操作：

    • 定义一个$user变量，其中包含具有给定电子邮件的用户。
    • 检查$user是否已注册，如果未注册，则返回'msg' => 'incorrect username or password'和401状态码。

    注意：401 状态码表示用户未经授权。

    接下来，如果电子邮件通过上述检查，则生成一个令牌，如下所示：

     $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ;

    上面的代码会生成一个用于登录的令牌。

    接下来，创建将发送回用户的响应，如下所示：

     $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 );

    上面的代码创建了一个名为$res的变量，它是一个包含创建的user和生成的token的数组，并使用response函数返回它以及状态码201 ，这意味着创建了一个资源，在本例中为令牌。 现在login功能应该如下所示：

     public function login ( Request $request ) { $data = $request -> validate ([ 'email' => 'required|string' , 'password' => 'required|string' ]); $user = User :: where ( 'email' , $data [ 'email' ]) -> first (); if ( ! $user || ! Hash :: check ( $data [ 'password' ], $user -> password )) { return response ([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401 ); } $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ; $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 ); }

    接下来，为上述函数创建一个登录路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/login' , [ AuthController :: class , 'login' ]);

    注意：这条路线将是公开的。

    您现在可以通过将注册用户的电子邮件和密码发送到/login路由来登录，如下所示：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由的 URL： localhost:8000/api/login 。
    • 红框是我发送给服务器的form-data格式的数据。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果 - 这将是登录用户和生成的令牌。 接下来，将生成的令牌添加为不记名令牌，中提琴！ 您现在已通过身份验证，可以访问受保护的路由。

    接下来，我将向您展示如何创建注销功能

    登出

    在我们的例子中，登录函数是所有AuthController函数中最简单的。

    首先，创建一个公共logout功能，如下所示：

     public function logout ( Request $request ) { }

    接下来，您需要删除用户的有效令牌，您可以这样做：

     auth () -> user () -> tokens () -> delete (); return [ 'message' => 'user logged out' ];

    上述函数删除了登录用户的令牌，这意味着不记名令牌将不再起作用，用户将未经身份验证，并返回'message' => 'user logged out' 。

    现在，为注销功能创建一个路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/logout' , [ AuthController :: class , 'logout' ]);

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/logout的 URL。
    • 橙色框是我发送到服务器的令牌类型，即bearer token 。
    • 蓝色框是我发送到服务器的令牌，这是我登录时生成的令牌。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果——这将是从logout功能返回的消息。

    重组路线

    成功实现Signup、Login、Logout功能后，接下来就是将保护路由和公共路由分开。

    在这种情况下，您需要删除之前用于对CRUD路由进行分组的resource方法，因为create 、 update和delete路由现在将受到保护，因为未经身份验证的用户不应该能够创建、更新或删除帖子。

    所以api.php文件现在看起来像这样：

     // signup and login Route :: post ( '/signup' , [ AuthController :: class , 'sign_up' ]); Route :: post ( '/login' , [ AuthController :: class , 'login' ]); // public post routes Route :: get ( '/posts/search/{title}' , [ PostController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/post/author/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'get_author' ]); // public author routes Route :: get ( '/authors/search/{name}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/author/posts/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'get_posts' ]); // private posts and authors routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { // private post routes Route :: post ( '/posts' , [ PostController :: class , 'store' ]); Route :: put ( '/posts/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'update' ]); Route :: delete ( '/posts/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'destroy' ]); // private author routes Route :: post ( '/authors' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'store' ]); Route :: put ( '/authors/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'update' ]); Route :: delete ( '/authors/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'destroy' ]); // logout Route :: post ( '/logout' , [ AuthController :: class , 'logout' ]); });

    就是这样！您已经成功实现了 API 的身份验证部分。 在下一部分中，我将向您展示如何在 Laravel 中测试 API。

    请使用评论部分的建议和反馈，我真的很感激。 我希望你喜欢这篇文章！

    这个系列的所有代码都可以在这里找到



原文:

In this part of the series, you'll learn the following:

  • What Sanctum is
  • How to install and use Laravel Sanctum
  • Implement the Sign-Up function
  • Implement the Login function
  • Implement the Logout function
  • Restructure the routes to protected and public

Laravel Sanctum setup

Laravel Sanctum, also commonly known as Sanctum is a lightweight authentication system used to authenticate token-based APIs and SPAs (ReactJs, VueJs, etc). In this section, I will show you how to authenticate users with Sanctum.

Install Sanctum

Due to Laravel's aim to provide a great developer experience, the Laravel project you generated in the first part of the series includes Sanctum, and you can confirm that by going to composer.json file, and it should be inside the require array like so:


The green box is the require array. If you can't find Sanctum inside the array in your composer.json file, run the following command to install it:

composer require laravel/sanctum

The above command will install Sanctum inside your app, and you can confirm by checking the composer.json file again.

Create personal access tokens migration

After confirming Sanctum's installation, the next thing is to create a personal access tokens table in the database, you do that by publishing Sanctum's configurations and migrations file by running the following in your command line:

php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"

The above command will create a create_personal_access_tokens_table.php in your /database/migrations folder and a sanctum.php file inside the /config folder, once you have verified the creation of those two files, the next thing to do is to migrate the new migration file, and you do that with the following command:

The above command will add a new personal_access_tokens table to your database, check your database manager to verify:

Next, go to the app/Http/Kernel.php file and replace the api array inside the middlewareGroups array with the following code:

'api' => [ \Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\EnsureFrontendRequestsAreStateful::class,
'throttle:api',
\Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\SubstituteBindings::class, ],

The above code is the middleware that will be used to authenticate our API.

Next, I'll show you can how to protect routes in Laravel.

Protecting Routes

To protect your routes, you need to group the protected routes with a middleware function like so:

// posts routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
// protected routes go here
});

the above code uses the static group function on the Route Facade and adds the middleware array that utilizes the 'auth:sanctum' middleware to protect the routes that you define inside the function. To show you how this works, I'll add all the post routes inside the function like so:

// posts routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
Route::resource('posts', PostController::class);
Route::get('/posts/search/{title}', [PostController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/post/author/{id}', [PostController::class, 'get_author']);
});

Now try to get all posts by making a GET request to localhost:8000/api/posts and you should get the following result:

The green box is the result you would get from the request, and it reads "message": "Unauthenticated.", and that's it! the route has been protected successfully, Now you need to define the steps the user has to take to get authenticated. Next, we will define the signup function.

Note: The above is just an example, I'm going to restructure all the routes later.

Next, I'll show you how to set up a controller for the functions related to authentication.

AuthController

You learned in the second part of the series that controllers are used to organizing functions in your application, So you'll need to create a controller that will contain all the functions related to authentication.

First, create a controller with artisan, name it AuthController like so:

php artisan make:controller AuthController

Note: You should not add the --resource flag, as we won't be using the CRUD functionality here.

That should create a controller file that contains the following code:

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request; class AuthController extends Controller
{ //
}

Next, add the dependencies required which in this case will be:

use App\Models\User;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Response;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash;

Add the code above under the namespace App\Http\Controllers; line.

  • The User is the user model and migration that was created when you generated your Laravel application.
  • The Request is the object that contains any data the user sends to the server.
  • The Response is the object that contains any data the server sends back to the user.
  • The Hash contains bcrypt function that will be used to hash the passwords.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Sign-Up function

Sign Up

For users to be able to sign in, you need to create the function. So create a public sign_up function like so:

 public function sign_up(Request $request){ }

Next, validate the data coming through the request object like so:

 $data = $request->validate([ 'name' => 'required|string', 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email', 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]);

The above code validates the data using the validate function.

  • The name is a required string.
  • The email is a required string and has to be a unique value inside the column in the users table.
  • The password is a required string and needs to be confirmed, so the user needs to input it a second time.

Next, create user using the static create function on the User model like so:

 $user = User::create([ 'name' => $data['name'], 'email' => $data['email'], 'password' => bcrypt($data['password']) ]);

The above code uses the create function with an array of the previous data variable to create a user.

Note: The password in the above array is wrapped in bcrypt function, so the password will be hashed before saving the user to the database.

Next, generate an authentication token using the createToken function on the $user like so:

$token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken;

The above code will create a token that will be sent along with every request to a protected route.

Next, create the response that will be sent back once the user has been created successfully:

 $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201);

The above code created a variable named $res which is an array that contains the created user and the generated token, and returns it using the response function along with the status code 201 which means that a resource was created, which is the user and the token. Now the sign_up function should look like so:

 public function sign_up(Request $request){ $data = $request->validate([ 'name' => 'required|string', 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email', 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]); $user = User::create([ 'name' => $data['name'], 'email' => $data['email'], 'password' => bcrypt($data['password']) ]); $token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken; $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201); }

Next, create a signup route for the above function like so:

Route::post('/signup', [AuthController::class, 'sign_up']);

Note: This route will be public.

You can now create a user by sending the required data to the /signup route, like so:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/signup.
  • The red box is the data I sent to the server in form-data format.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the user that was created and the generated token.

Next, add the generated token as the bearer token and send a GET request to the protected routes you defined earlier:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a GET request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/posts.
  • The orange box is the type of token I sent to the server which is the bearer token.
  • The blue box is the token I sent to the server which is the token that was generated when I signed up(this is why you get logged in automatically once you sign up on any application).
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the posts in the database which was unavailable earlier because I was not authenticated.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Sign-In function.

Sign In

You need to create a login function so users can log in. To do so, create a login function like so:

 public function login(Request $request) { }

Next, validate the request data like so:

 $data = $request->validate([ 'email' => 'required|string', 'password' => 'required|string' ]);
  • The email is a required string.
  • The password is a required string.

Next, check if the user is registered like so:

 $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first(); if (!$user || !Hash::check($data['password'], $user->password)) { return response([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401); }

The above code does the following:

  • Define a $user variable that contains the user with the given email.
  • Check if the $user is registered and return 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' with a 401 status code if it isn't.

Note: 401 status code means the user is unauthorized.

Next, generate a token if the email passes the above check, like so:

$token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken;

The above code generates a token that will be used to log in.

Next, create the response that will be sent back to the user like so:

 $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201);

The above code created a variable named $res which is an array that contains the created user and the generated token, and returns it using the response function along with the status code 201 which means that a resource was created, in this case the token. Now the login function should look like so:

 public function login(Request $request) { $data = $request->validate([ 'email' => 'required|string', 'password' => 'required|string' ]); $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first(); if (!$user || !Hash::check($data['password'], $user->password)) { return response([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401); } $token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken; $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201); }

Next, create a login route for the above function like so:

Route::post('/login', [AuthController::class, 'login']);

Note: This route will be public.

You can now log in by sending the email and password of a registered user to the /login route, like so:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route: localhost:8000/api/login.
  • The red box is the data I sent to the server in form-data format.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the logged-in user and the generated token. Next, add the generated token as the bearer token, and viola! you are now authenticated and can visit protected routes.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Logout function

Logout

The login function is the simplest of all the AuthController functions in our case.

First, create a public logout function like so:

 public function logout(Request $request) { }

Next, you need to delete the user's valid token, and you do that like so:

 auth()->user()->tokens()->delete(); return [ 'message' => 'user logged out' ];

The above function deletes the token for a logged-in user, which means the bearer token will no longer work and the user will be unauthenticated, and returns 'message' => 'user logged out'.

Now, create a route for the logout function like so:

 Route::post('/logout', [AuthController::class, 'logout']);

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/logout.
  • The orange box is the type of token I sent to the server which is the bearer token.
  • The blue box is the token I sent to the server which is the token that was generated when I logged in.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the message that was returned from the logout function.

Restructuring the routes

After the Signup, Login, and Logout functions have been implemented successfully, the next thing is to separate the protected routes and public routes.

In this case it will be required that you remove the resource method that you used to group the CRUD routes earlier because the create, update and delete routes will now be protected because an unauthenticated user should not be able to create, update or delete posts.

So the api.php file will look like so now:

// signup and login
Route::post('/signup', [AuthController::class, 'sign_up']);
Route::post('/login', [AuthController::class, 'login']); // public post routes
Route::get('/posts/search/{title}', [PostController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/post/author/{id}', [PostController::class, 'get_author']); // public author routes
Route::get('/authors/search/{name}', [AuthorController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/author/posts/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'get_posts']); // private posts and authors routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
// private post routes
Route::post('/posts', [PostController::class, 'store']);
Route::put('/posts/{id}', [PostController::class, 'update']);
Route::delete('/posts/{id}', [PostController::class, 'destroy']);
// private author routes
Route::post('/authors', [AuthorController::class, 'store']);
Route::put('/authors/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'update']);
Route::delete('/authors/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'destroy']); // logout 
Route::post('/logout', [AuthController::class, 'logout']);
});

And that's it!, you have successfully implemented the authentication part of the API. In the next part, I will show you how to test APIs in Laravel.

Please use the comment section for suggestions and feedback, I would really appreciate that. I hope you enjoyed the article!

All the code for this series can be found here
item.author: olodocoder

item.title: 使用 react-router 响应顺风响应侧边栏和暗模式 | React tailwind responsive sidebar & dark mode with react-router
item.description:
马利克-伊德里斯

发表于我正在寻找一个带有响应式侧边栏的简单布局，并支持使用 react 和 tailwind 构建的暗模式。 我决定自己创造一个。

它支持明暗模式，并带有响应式侧边栏。 暂时没有手机菜单！
直播网站

没有使用第三方库！

随意以任何合适的方式贡献和扩展它:)

这个项目是用Create React App引导的。

可用脚本

在项目目录中，您可以运行：

npm start

在开发模式下运行应用程序。
打开http://localhost:3000在浏览器中查看。

当您进行更改时，该页面将重新加载。
您可能还会在控制台中看到任何 lint 错误。

npm test

在交互式监视模式下启动测试运行程序。
有关更多信息，请参阅有关运行测试的部分。

npm run build

将用于生产的应用程序构建到build文件夹。
它在生产模式下正确捆绑 React 并优化构建以获得最佳性能。

构建被缩小并且文件名包含哈希。
您的应用已准备好部署！

有关更多信息，请参阅有关部署的部分。

npm run eject

注意：这是一种单向操作。 一旦你eject ，你就不能回去了！

如果你…

与我联系:)
领英



原文:

Malik-Idrees

Posted on May 15

I was searching for a simple layout with responsive sidebar and support for dark mode built with react and tailwind. And I decided to create one myself.

It has support for light and dark mode and comes with a responsive side bar. No mobile menu for now!
Live Website

No third party libraries used!

Feel free to contribute and extend it in any suitable way :)

This project was bootstrapped with Create React App.

Available Scripts

In the project directory, you can run:

npm start

Runs the app in the development mode.
Open http://localhost:3000 to view it in your browser.

The page will reload when you make changes.
You may also see any lint errors in the console.

npm test

Launches the test runner in the interactive watch mode.
See the section about running tests for more information.

npm run build

Builds the app for production to the build folder.
It correctly bundles React in production mode and optimizes the build for the best performance.

The build is minified and the filenames include the hashes.
Your app is ready to be deployed!

See the section about deployment for more information.

npm run eject

Note: this is a one-way operation. Once you eject, you can't go back!

If you…

item.title: 使用 node 和 nginx Dockerize 一个 React 应用程序以及如何将图像推送到 Docker Hub | Dockerize a React App with node and nginx and how to push an image to Docker Hub
item.description:
让我们看看一个 React 应用程序 dockerized 以及如何将我们要创建的图像推送到 Docker Hub。

首先，如果您需要搜索主要的 docker 命令，请查看： https ://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline

您需要创建一个具有写入、读取和删除权限的特定用户。 看看https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/access-tokens/

在 docker hub 上创建访问令牌后：

 docker login -u <username>

创建一个 React 应用程序：

 npx create-app <app-name>

我们将使用https://hub.docker.com/_/node ，进入 src 文件夹，我们可以创建 Dockerfile 并复制这些命令。

 FROM node AS prod WORKDIR /app COPY package*.json ./ RUN npm install COPY . . # RUN npm test - if you want to test before to build RUN npm run build FROM nginx:alpine AS prod WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx/html COPY --from=prod /app/build . EXPOSE 80 # run nginx with global directives and daemon off ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

进入 src 文件夹，您可以创建 .dockerignore 并添加它：

执行 Docker 镜像构建：

 docker image build -t react:v1 .

构建映像后，让我们运行以验证它是否正常工作。

 docker run -dit -p 3300:80 --name react react:v1

要获取容器 ID：

获得容器 ID 后，您可以使用它并提交容器：

 docker container commit <id-container> react:v1

让我们查找我们之前构建的图像并获取 id：

继续，我们需要提交：

 docker container commit 49cc7b3e3682 react:v1

查看docker images命令的输出，我们可以看到存储库名称、标签和图像 ID。 我们需要创建一个标签：

 docker tag <image-name:tag> <your-repository>/image-name:image-name-repository docker tag react:v1 luizcalaca/react:react

最后是推：

 docker push <repository>/<tag> docker push luizcalaca/react:react

联系人
电子邮件： luizcalaca@gmail.com
领英： https ://www.linkedin.com/in/luizcalaca/
推特： https ://twitter.com/luizcalaca



原文:

Let's see a React app dockerized and how to push the image the we are going to create to the Docker Hub.

First if you need to search the main docker commands, look at: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline

You need to create one specific user with the write, read and delete privileges. Look at https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/access-tokens/

After creating an access token on docker hub:

docker login -u <username>

Creating a React App with:

npx create-app <app-name>

We are going to use https://hub.docker.com/_/node, into the src folder we can create the Dockerfile and copy these commands.

FROM node AS prod
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
# RUN npm test - if you want to test before to build
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine AS prod
WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY --from=prod /app/build .
EXPOSE 80
# run nginx with global directives and daemon off
ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

Into src folder, you can create the .dockerignore and add it:

Execute the Docker image build:

docker image build -t react:v1 .

After you built an image, let’s run to verify if it is working.

docker run -dit -p 3300:80 --name react react:v1

To get the container id:

After you got the container id you can use it and commit the container:

docker container commit <id-container> react:v1

Let’s looking for the image that we built before and get the id:

Going on, we need to commit:

docker container commit 49cc7b3e3682 react:v1

Looking at the output of the docker images command, we can see the repository name, tag and image id. We need to create a tag:

docker tag <image-name:tag> <your-repository>/image-name:image-name-repository

docker tag react:v1 luizcalaca/react:react

The last is the push:

docker push <repository>/<tag>

docker push luizcalaca/react:react

Contacts
Email: luizcalaca@gmail.com
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/luizcalaca
Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/luizcalaca/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/luizcalaca
item.author: luizcalaca
